@@ -851,7 +851,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_networkx-w7ge","title":"NX-REPLACE: Misc algorithms — euler, cycles, hashing, coloring, similarity, etc.","description":"## Functions to replace (22)
Euler/structural:
1. eulerize (__init__.py:6123) — add edges to make graph Eulerian
2. moral_graph (__init__.py:6133) — marry parents in DAG
3. minimum_cycle_basis (__init__.py:6160) — Horton's algorithm
4. chordless_cycles (__init__.py:6169) — enumerate chordless cycles

Graph hashing:
5. weisfeiler_lehman_graph_hash (__init__.py:6454) — WL hash for isomorphism
6. weisfeiler_lehman_subgraph_hashes (__init__.py:6463) — WL subgraph hashes

Coloring:
7. equitable_color (__init__.py:6800) — equitable graph coloring
8. chromatic_polynomial (__init__.py:6808) — deletion-contraction

Similarity/distance:
9. panther_similarity (__init__.py:7001) — random-walk similarity
10. panther_vector_similarity (__init__.py:7170) — vector variant
11. cd_index (__init__.py:7229) — consolidation-disruption index

Misc:
12. local_bridges (__init__.py:1345) — edges whose removal increases distance
13. complete_to_chordal_graph (__init__.py:6938) — chordal completion
14. effective_graph_resistance (__init__.py:7201) — Kirchhoff index
15. goldberg_radzik (__init__.py:7237) — shortest paths with negative weights
16. communicability_exp (__init__.py:7162) — matrix exponential
17. local_constraint (__init__.py:7123) — Burt's constraint measure
18. is_perfect_graph (__init__.py:7486) — perfect graph recognition
19. k_factor (__init__.py:7541) — k-regular spanning subgraph
20. spectral_graph_forge (__init__.py:7550) — spectral graph generation
21. mycielskian (__init__.py:7293) — Mycielski graph construction
22. random_powerlaw_tree_sequence (__init__.py:8299) — power-law degree sequence

## Priority tiers
- Tier 1 (moderate complexity, good value): eulerize, moral_graph, WL hashes, mycielskian
- Tier 2 (complex algorithms): minimum_cycle_basis, equitable_color, chromatic_polynomial
- Tier 3 (exotic, OK to keep as NX): spectral_graph_forge, panther_similarity, effective_graph_resistance

## Files to modify
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs: add Rust implementations
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: add bindings
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewire

## Acceptance criteria
- Each replaced function matches NX output
- Tier 3 functions may remain as NX delegation if complexity is prohibitive","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:16.777801946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:42:08.015140569Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:42:08.014895194Z","close_reason":"Superseded: split into 3 smaller beads (misc-tier1, misc-tier2, misc-tier3) for better parallelism and clearer scope","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-wd3s","title":"parity: Graph and DiGraph should support number_of_edges(u, v)","description":"Graph.number_of_edges() and DiGraph.number_of_edges() currently reject endpoint arguments with TypeError, while upstream NetworkX supports number_of_edges(u, v) and returns 0 for a missing edge query like ('a', 'missing'). Match the upstream endpoint-query contract on Graph and DiGraph.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:50.585257105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:50.585257105Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-wf4","title":"Add Python tests for new graph metric and connectivity bindings","description":"Write Python tests for the 12 new bindings added in franken_networkx-a0n: volume, boundary_expansion, conductance, edge_expansion, node_expansion, mixing_expansion, non_edges, average_node_connectivity, is_k_edge_connected, all_pairs_dijkstra, number_of_spanning_arborescences, global_node_connectivity. Cross-validate against NetworkX reference.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:20:17.046394658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T03:21:58.185038420Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T03:21:58.184400050Z","close_reason":"Added 42 Python tests cross-validating 12 new bindings against NetworkX reference","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-wfg5","title":"Restore corona_product directed error-contract parity","description":"networkx.corona_product raises NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for directed type') when G is directed, but still raises the directedness mismatch NetworkXError when only H is directed. FrankenNetworkX currently routes both mixed-direction cases through _validate_product_graph_types and loses the directed-type error contract for directed G. Fix the wrapper ordering and add parity tests for DG, GD, and DD input combinations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:44:13.638530672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:46:12.780690985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:46:12.778652587Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -2670,3 +2670,28 @@ def test_reverse_view_supports_subgraph_and_copy_like_networkx_without_fallback(
     assert result_subgraph_copy.is_directed() == expected_subgraph_copy.is_directed()
     assert result_subgraph_copy.is_multigraph() == expected_subgraph_copy.is_multigraph()
     assert _graph_snapshot(result_subgraph_copy) == _graph_snapshot(expected_subgraph_copy)
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    ("fnx_cls", "nx_cls"),
+    [
+        (fnx.DiGraph, nx.DiGraph),
+        (fnx.MultiDiGraph, nx.MultiDiGraph),
+    ],
+)
+def test_reverse_view_supports_edge_subgraph_without_fallback(monkeypatch, fnx_cls, nx_cls):
+    graph, expected = _view_utility_graph_pair(fnx_cls, nx_cls)
+    if graph.is_multigraph():
+        selected_edges = [("b", "a", 1)]
+    else:
+        selected_edges = [("b", "a")]
+    expected_result = nx.reverse_view(expected).edge_subgraph(selected_edges)
+
+    _block_networkx_utilities(monkeypatch, "reverse_view", "edge_subgraph")
+
+    result = fnx.reverse_view(graph).edge_subgraph(selected_edges)
+
+    assert result.is_directed() == expected_result.is_directed()
+    assert result.is_multigraph() == expected_result.is_multigraph()
+    assert result.frozen == expected_result.frozen
+    assert _graph_snapshot(result) == _graph_snapshot(expected_result)
